title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California law allows counties to establish advisory or independent redistricting commissions.",
    "If a county does not use an independent citizens redistricting commission, the incumbent county supervisors draw their own district boundaries, potentially leading to partisan conflicts or favoritism.",
    "Several counties in California have established Independent Citizens Redistricting Commissions.",
    "AB 34 would require Orange County to establish an independent commission to draw its county supervisorial districts for the 2030 redistricting cycle.",
    "The independent commission would be reconstituted after every census for subsequent redistricting cycles.",
    "The partisan makeup of the commission would be proportional to the makeup of Orange County voters.",
    "AB 34 includes eligibility criteria and restrictions for commission members.",
    "AB 34 adds provisions for the removal of commissioners and appointing alternates.",
    "AB 34 mandates translation of outreach materials in applicable languages.",
    "AB 34 sets attendance expectations for commission members.",
    "AB 34 prohibits commissioners from endorsing or contributing to an elective office of the local jurisdiction.",
    "The use of an independent citizens redistricting commission would impact political fairness in representing the diversity and interests of the community.",
    "Independent redistricting commissions are considered a proven way to create fair maps.",
    "Establishing an Orange County Independent Redistricting Commission aims to center community voices in neighborhood representation over special interests.",
    "AB 34 aims to remove conflicts of interest for supervisors involved in redrawing political district lines."
  ]
}
```
